title: pyrrocoma lagopus
intro: Pyrrocoma lagopus, also known as rabbit's foot or mountain sunshine, is a perennial plant native to the western United States and Canada, characterized by its yellow flowers and woolly leaves. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. Research suggests that Pyrrocoma lagopus may have health benefits, including reducing inflammation and alleviating respiratory issues, although more studies are needed to confirm its efficacy. Traditionally, the plant is prepared for herbal use by making infusions or decoctions from its roots and leaves, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
